title: Character sexuality hot buttons pressed here
display_title: Character sexuality hot buttons pressed here
wikitext:
[]: Post by Eric Blair » Tue Jun 29, 2010 5:49 pm TehDonutKing wrote: Can't we just assume that everyone is bi unless stated otherwise?

[]: Post by oOoOoOo » Tue Jun 29, 2010 8:54 pm Certainly it is possible to sympathize with Gendo, when we're talking about relationships, love, and all that jazz. If we're talking about human rights, well... ^^; As for sexuality theories , we really can't make assumptions about any of the characters. We can argue "Shinji is attracted to Asuka" but we cannot argue "Shinji is only attracted to girls". Kinsey says there's just as good a chance he's bi as straight, but his orientation doesn't matter to the story. It's best to talk about the individuals a character is attracted to. (I have no qualms with shipping. I "ship" Asuka x Shinji. Did I say that right?) This really applies to reality as well. If I see a boy and a girl holding hands, it isn't for sure that they're both straight. I can say that the boy is into girls and the girl is into boys, though.

[]: Post by Synapsid » Tue Jun 29, 2010 8:54 pm Lucretius wrote: ...NGE isn't nearly as morally ambiguous as it's made at to be...

[]: Post by Eric Blair » Tue Jun 29, 2010 8:57 pm Synapsid wrote: troll

[]: Post by Synapsid » Tue Jun 29, 2010 9:07 pm Eric Blair wrote: Oh look, my stalker is here.

[]: Post by Eric Blair » Tue Jun 29, 2010 9:16 pm Synapsid wrote: Troll

[]: Post by Sachi » Tue Jun 29, 2010 9:55 pm Synapsid : Making your text smaller doesn't lessen the amount of trollfeeding you're doing. Don't feed the troll, period. Eric Blair : that's enough. Last warning for this thread.

If we're talking about human rights, well... ^^; As for sexuality theories , we really can't make assumptions about any of the characters. We can argue "Shinji is attracted to Asuka" but we cannot argue "Shinji is only attracted to girls". Kinsey says there's just as good a chance he's bi as straight, but his orientation doesn't matter to the story. It's best to talk about the individuals a character is attracted to. (I have no qualms with shipping. I "ship" Asuka x Shinji. Did I say that right?) This really applies to reality as well. If I see a boy and a girl holding hands, it isn't for sure that they're both straight. I can say that the boy is into girls and the girl is into boys, though.
